protocol.gt_isolation: proof bodies replaced with admit() before agent launch

v4_minimal.fail_modes: Various compilation and verification errors across all functions

sibling_effect.description: Comparing any sibling variant (v1/v2/v3) vs no-sibling (v4)
sibling_effect.sibling_contribution: 67 percentage points — siblings are ESSENTIAL, not optional
sibling_effect.note: V4 (minimal) failed ALL 6 functions. Without sibling context, the model cannot generate correct Verus proofs even for the easiest functions.

prompt_ordering_effect.description: V1, V2, V3 all achieve 4/6 but on DIFFERENT function subsets
v2_unique_passes: F4_medium (pass in v2, v3 but not v1)
v3_unique_passes: F6_hard (pass in v1, v3 but not v2)
prompt_ordering_effect.conclusion: Prompt ordering affects WHICH functions pass, not HOW MANY. Different orderings have complementary strengths — suggesting a retry strategy with different prompts.

difficulty_correlation.easy_F1_F2: pass in all sibling variants (3/3 each), fail in v4
difficulty_correlation.medium_F3_F4: mixed — each passes in 2-3 of 3 sibling variants
difficulty_correlation.hard_F5: FAIL in all variants (0/4) — ceiling test
difficulty_correlation.hard_F6: passes in v1, v3 but not v2 — surprising non-monotonicity

analysis.key_finding: Siblings are the single most important context component. Prompt ordering is secondary but creates complementary coverage — the UNION of v1+v2+v3 passes would be 5/6 (83%), better than any single variant.

evaluation.winner: TIE (v1, v2, v3 all 4/6) — but v2 produces most concise proofs (avg 27 LOC)
evaluation.recommended_pipeline_strategy: Try v1 first, retry with v2 on failure, then v3. This covers 5/6.

F1_easy_v4.root_cause: Without sibling showing lemma_neg_square_eq + lemma_square_mod_noop pattern, model guesses wrong lemma name

F2_easy_v4.root_cause: Missing p_gt_2() call. All sibling variants include this because siblings show the pattern. Without siblings, model omits the prerequisite.
F2_easy_v4.code_was_correct_except_for: p_gt_2() — the 3 lemma calls were identical to the passing variants

F3_medium_v4.root_cause: Complex assertion structure without sibling template to follow

F4_medium_v4.root_cause: Type/argument mismatch in lemma calls without sibling demonstrating correct casting

F5_hard_v4.root_cause: Hallucinated lemma_pow2_plus_one (does not exist); used 2int literal (invalid Verus syntax)

F6_hard_v4.root_cause: Trigger inference failure on quantifier assertion

v4_failure_analysis.key_insight: V4 failures are NOT about wrong strategy — the model identifies the right proof approach. Failures come from: (1) hallucinated lemma names (without sibling examples showing correct names), (2) missing boilerplate prerequisites like p_gt_2(), (3) Verus syntax errors (int literal suffixes, trigger annotations). Siblings provide working SYNTAX templates, not just proof ideas.
